title: Three Bears
pages: Once upon a time, there were three bears who lived in a house in the forest. There was a great big father bear, a middle-sized mother bear, and a little baby bear.
One morning, their breakfast porridge was too hot, so they went for a walk in the forest while it cooled.
Along came a little {m:boy|f:girl} called {childName}. {m:He|f:She} knocked on the door, and when no one answered, {m:he|f:she} walked right in.

On the table were three bowls of porridge. {childName} tasted the porridge from the great big bowl. «This porridge is too hot!» {m:he|f:she} said. Then {m:he|f:she} tasted the porridge from the middle-sized bowl. «This porridge is too cold!» {m:he|f:she} said.
Then {m:he|f:she} tasted the porridge from the little bowl. «This porridge is just right!» {m:he|f:she} said, and ate it all up.
